leaf_properties.label: min value
leaf_properties.description: This input identifies the minimum value of the range associated with a data structure. The value may be a scalar or a reference to another object. The disjoint sub-properties of this data:value distinguish between scalar values and object references.

leaf_properties.label: max value
leaf_properties.description: This input identifies the maximum value of the range associated with a data structure. The value may be a scalar or a reference to another object. The disjoint sub-properties of this data:value distinguish between scalar values and object references.

leaf_properties.label: Select a unit
leaf_properties.description: A unit of measure, or unit, is a particular quantity value that has been chosen as a scale for measuring other quantities the same kind (more generally of equivalent dimension).For example, the meter is a quantity of length that has been rigorously defined and standardized by the BIPM (International Board of Weights and Measures).Any measurement of the length can be expressed as a number multiplied by the unit meter.More formally, the value of a physical quantity Q with respect to a unit (U) is expressed as the scalar multiple of a real number (n) and U, as  $Q = nU$.
